title: PHP Numbers
content:
### PHP Numbers

PHP supports integers and floating-point numbers.

### Integer

An integer is a whole number without decimals.

| Property | Details |
|----------|---------|
| Range | Platform-dependent (typically 64-bit) |
| Formats | Decimal, hexadecimal (`0x`), octal (`0o`), binary (`0b`) |
| Check | `is_int()` |

### Float

A float (floating-point number) has decimal points or uses exponential form.

```php
<?php
$x = 10;       // int
$y = 10.5;     // float
$z = 2.5e3;    // 2500 (float)
$bin = 0b1010; // 10 (binary)
$hex = 0xFF;   // 255 (hex)

echo is_int($x);   // true
echo is_float($y); // true
?>
```

### Number Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `abs()` | Absolute value |
| `round()` | Rounds a float |
| `ceil()` | Rounds up |
| `floor()` | Rounds down |
| `min()` | Minimum of values |
| `max()` | Maximum of values |
| `pow()` | Exponentiation |
| `sqrt()` | Square root |

> **Key Point:** PHP automatically converts between int and float as needed. Use `is_numeric()` to check if a value is a number or numeric string.
codeExample:
<?php
$a = 15.7;
echo round($a);  // 16
echo ceil($a);   // 16
echo floor($a);  // 15

echo max(10, 20, 30); // 30
echo min(10, 20, 30); // 10
echo sqrt(25);        // 5
echo pow(2, 3);       // 8
?>

quiz.question: Which function rounds a number UP to the nearest integer?
